prompt:
Read the workflow below and follow it. Task ID: GROVE-S03-T07.

---

## Prior phase summaries (carry-forward)

### plan
Objective: Implement grove doctor with a new core::harness module (sentinel constants + mode→content tables), core::doctor (pure diagnose with universal + explore checks), and Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New core/src/harness.rs: GROVE_START, GROVE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY, expected_mcp_args(Mode), expected_claude_marker(Mode), agents_md_expected(Mode) — resolves constant-ownership blocker.; cli/src/init.rs refactored: remove local CLAUDE_START/CLAUDE_END/MCP_SERVER_KEY consts; import from grove_core::harness — sentinel strings live in exactly one place.; New core/src/doctor.rs: Status{Ok,Warn,Fail,Info}, Check{group,name,status,detail,hint}, Report{mode,checks}, Report::ok(), diagnose(root,force)->Report — pure read-only.; core/src/lib.rs: add pub mod harness and pub mod doctor.; Universal checks: grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, four harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.; Harness sub-checks use harness::expected_mcp_args/expected_claude_marker/agents_md_expected; validate on-disk files vs declared mode; Fail on drift.; Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only): explore_config_valid, provider_reachable, model_served, allowed_tools_known, tap_config.; cli/src/main.rs: Cmd::Doctor{path,explore,standard}; dispatch arm; grouped ✓/⚠/✗ human output or --json; exit code from Report::ok().; Unit tests in core/src/doctor.rs build all fixtures by hand (tempdir + std::fs::write) — resolves test-strategy blocker (no cross-crate private helpers).; Harness drift matrix: per-mode clean fixture → all harness checks Ok; selected drift scenarios → correct Fail/Warn.; warn_only_report_exits_zero: Report::ok() true when only Warn/Info present — AC 9 warning boundary (per reviewer advisory).; Integration tests: doctor --help, clean project exits 0, --json parses, induced drift exits 1 under both output modes.
Full artifact: PLAN.md
### review_plan
Objective: Re-reviewed the revised grove doctor plan (core::harness + core::doctor + Cmd::Doctor) to confirm the two prior blockers and one advisory were resolved and all reused seams exist.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Blocker 1 (constant ownership) resolved: new core/src/harness.rs is single source of truth; init.rs migrates to import it — no core->cli cycle, no duplication.; Verified on-disk init.rs owns CLAUDE_START/END (l.20-21) and MCP_SERVER_KEY (l.79); plan's per-mode markers match real steering blocks (outline/skill/explore).; Blocker 2 (cross-crate private test helpers) resolved: unit tests build tempdir fixtures via std::fs::write, no reconcile_harness/seed_lock imports.; Advisory (warn-only exits zero) incorporated as warn_only_report_exits_zero; Report::ok() correctly keys only off Status::Fail.; Seam active_mode(root, ModeChoice)->Mode confirmed (config.rs:291); ModeChoice None/ForceExplore/ForceStandard matches --explore/--standard precedence.; verify_lock (registry.rs:465), health_probe + HealthError{Unreachable,ModelMissing} (client.rs), list_models, ExploreConfig::validate, toolset consts, registry helpers all present.; Mode enum {Mcp,Skill,Both,McpLlm,Grammars} matches expected_mcp_args mapping; global --json flag present (main.rs:24) so AC6 dual-format exit code is implementable.; Advisory (non-blocking): harness_serve_surface is Info-only; acceptable since surface is derived and .mcp.json drift is already a hard Fail. No live determine_surface seam — compute inline.; Advisory (non-blocking): ensure explore/standard bool->ModeChoice conversion mirrors serve; decide+test precedence when both flags passed.
Full artifact: PLAN_REVIEW.md
### implementation
Objective: Implement grove doctor with core::harness (sentinel constants), core::doctor (pure diagnose), and Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New core/src/harness.rs: GROVE_START, GROVE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY, expected_mcp_args(Mode), expected_claude_marker(Mode), agents_md_expected(Mode) — single source of truth.; cli/src/init.rs: removed local CLAUDE_START/CLAUDE_END/MCP_SERVER_KEY consts; imported from grove_core::harness — sentinel strings in exactly one place.; New core/src/doctor.rs: Status{Ok,Warn,Fail,Info}, Check, Report, Report::ok(), diagnose(root,force)->Report — pure read-only using core primitives.; core/src/lib.rs: added pub mod harness and pub mod doctor (alphabetically ordered).; Universal checks (11): grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, four harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.; Harness sub-checks compare on-disk state to harness::expected_* functions; Fail on drift with grove init --as <mode> hint.; Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only): explore_config_valid, provider_reachable, model_served, allowed_tools_known, tap_config.; cli/src/main.rs: Cmd::Doctor{path,explore,standard}; grouped human output or --json; exit 1 from Report::ok() under both formats.; Unit tests in harness.rs: 3 coverage tests for all Mode variants across all three functions.; Unit tests in doctor.rs: warn_only exits zero, harness matrix all 5 modes, 4 drift scenarios, lock absent, explore absent, provider unreachable.; Integration tests in cli/tests/cli.rs: doctor --help, clean project exits 0, --json valid, drift exits nonzero (human + json).
Full artifact: PROGRESS.md
### code_review
Objective: Independently reviewed the grove doctor implementation (core::harness, core::doctor, Cmd::Doctor) against the approved PLAN and task ACs.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Isolated true T07 footprint via git diff HEAD: reconcile_harness in the cumulative diff is committed T04 work, not T07.; T07 changes: new harness.rs + doctor.rs, lib.rs module wiring, init.rs constant migration (8 lines, aliased GROVE_START/END), main.rs Cmd::Doctor, cli.rs integration tests.; Verified independently: cargo clippy -D warnings clean; cargo test --release --locked green (177 core + 92 CLI unit + 33 integration + 1 doc, 0 failed).; Read actual harness matrix/drift/explore tests — assertions discriminate real Ok/Warn/Fail, not tautologies.; AC1 shapes exact; Report.mode is core declared Mode not Surface; diagnose pure/read-only.; AC2 mode resolution via shared active_mode(root, force); --explore/--standard precedence correct.; AC3 universal checks present; harness drift -> Fail naming the file with grove init --as <mode> hint.; AC4 lock_integrity delegates to T06 verify_lock: Mismatch->Fail, Missing/absent->Warn (never Fail).; AC5 explore checks gated on McpLlm; HealthError hints surfaced verbatim; allowed_tools warn on unknown.; AC6 Cmd::Doctor + --json dual-format with exit code from Report::ok(); both formats integration-tested.; AC8 build/clippy clean, --help documents doctor, both new files newline-terminated.; Advisory only: Report.mode shows declared (not forced) mode in header; steering legality covered via validate(); cosmetic double blank line in init.rs.
Full artifact: CODE_REVIEW.md

# Validate Task
## Iron Laws

- Validate against the acceptance criteria as written; do not soften, expand, or reinterpret them. The validator's job is to catch what the implementer optimistically considered "done".
- Read `.forge/personas/qa-engineer.md` first; print the persona identity line (emoji, name, tagline) to stdout before any other tool use.
- All store I/O via `forge_store` (or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs`). Never edit `.forge/store/*.json` directly.

## Store-Write Verification

<!-- See _fragments/store-write-verification.md for the canonical block content -->

## Algorithm

```

0a. Pre-flight Gate Check:
   - **Entity-mode resolution:** read the kickoff arguments. `--task {id}` → `entity_kind = "task"`, `record_id = {id}`. `--bug {id}` → `entity_kind = "bug"`, `record_id = {id}`. All store-cli calls below substitute `{entity_kind}` and `{record_id}` for the literal "task"/{taskId} placeholders.
   - Run: `node .forge/tools/preflight-gate.cjs --phase validate --{entity_kind} {record_id}`
   - Exit 1 (gate failed) → print stderr and HALT. Do not proceed; do not attempt to produce the artifact.
   - Exit 2 (misconfiguration) → print stderr and HALT.
   - Exit 0 → continue.

0b. Pipeline Step Guard (user-invoked state check):
   - If `--force` is present in the invocation arguments, skip this step entirely.
   - If `entity_kind == "bug"`, skip this step entirely (bug state is managed by meta-fix-bug.md).
   - Read current task state:
     `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs read task {record_id} --fields status`
   - Extract the `status` field from the JSON output.
   - Allowed states for this phase: `implemented`, `review-approved`.
   - If the current status is NOT in the allowed set:
     Print the following and HALT (do not proceed):
     `× Task {record_id} is in state '{status}' — /forge:implement must complete first. To run the full pipeline: /forge:run-task {record_id}`

1. Read Review Loop Context:
   - Check the spawning prompt for a `### Review Loop Context` block.
   - If present, extract:
     - `Iteration: N of M` — current attempt number and the configured limit
     - `Is final iteration: true/false`
   - If absent (user-invoked, not orchestrated): treat as `iteration 1`, no limit — do
     NOT read any iteration cap from config. The orchestrator owns loop budgets; a human
     standalone re-run is the escape hatch for stuck items (forge-engineering#34).
   - Include `(iteration N of M)` (orchestrated) or `(standalone review)` in the opening line of the `VALIDATION_REPORT.md` artifact.
   - If this is the final iteration (`N == M`) and the verdict is `Revision Required`,
     append a `### Next Steps` section to the artifact showing:
     ```
     ### Next Steps
     - Force-approve (bypass remaining reviews): `/forge:approve --force {task_id}`
     - Increase iteration limit: edit `config.pipelines.{pipeline}.phases[validate].maxIterations`
     - Restart from validation: `/forge:validate {task_id}`
     ```

2. Load Context:
   - Read task prompt
   - Read approved PLAN.md
   - Read the implementation
   - Read PROGRESS.md

3. Validation:
   - Execute the "Acceptance Criteria" checklist from the plan
   - Verify that all technical constraints (e.g., performance, security) are met
   - Check for any regressions in related functionality
   - When re-running the test suite, use the **resolved test command** from `commands.test` in `.forge/config.json` (i.e. `` `${commands.test}` ``, e.g. `.venv/bin/python -m pytest`). Template placeholder: cargo test --release --locked. Do NOT invoke bare `python` / `python3` — the project interpreter is rarely on `$PATH`.

4. Verdict:
   - Write the validation report via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"validation-report", content:"<markdown>" })`
     The markdown content must use the format:
     **Verdict:** [Approved | Revision Required]
     - If Revision Required: list the failed criteria and required fixes
     - If Approved: confirm the task is validated
     - See step 1 for iteration header and final-iteration Next Steps requirements.

5. Finalize:
   - Update task status via `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs update-status task {taskId} status review-approved` (if Approved) or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs update-status task {taskId} status code-revision-required` (if Revision Required)
   - **Do NOT emit a phase event yourself.** The orchestrator owns event emission — it composes the canonical event from runtime telemetry (model, provider, tokens, wall times) plus the SUMMARY you write in the next step. Subagents that call `store-cli emit` for phase events hallucinate runtime facts (see Plan 11 / Slice 2). Write the SUMMARY and return.

6. Emit Summary Sidecar:
   - Write the validation summary via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"validation-summary", content:"<JSON>" })`
     The JSON content must have the following shape:
     ```json
     {
       "objective":   "<one sentence — what acceptance criteria were validated>",
       "findings":    ["<up to 12 bullets, 200 chars each — pass/fail per criterion>"],
       "verdict":     "<approved | revision>",
       "written_at":  "<current ISO 8601 timestamp>",
       "artifact_ref":"VALIDATION_REPORT.md"
     }
     ```
   - Call (the sidecar path is auto-resolved from the task record's `path` — never pass it):
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-summary {task_id} validation
     ```
   - If set-summary exits non-zero, fix the sidecar JSON and retry. Do not proceed without a valid summary.
```

## Friction Emit
Emit `type:friction` `{workflow:validate, persona:qa-engineer, issue}` per `_fragments/friction-emit.md`.

# GROVE-S03-T07: `grove doctor` — `core::doctor` report + CLI verb (checks, `--json`, exit code)
## Objective
## Acceptance Criteria
## Context
## Artifacts Involved
## Operational Impact
- **Version bump:** required at release (new user-facing verb).
- **Regeneration:** none; additive command.
- **Security scan:** not required.

[72 body lines omitted — 81 total]

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `core::harness` defines `GROVE_START`, `GROVE_END`, `MCP_SERVER_KEY`,
   `expected_mcp_args(Mode)`, `expected_claude_marker(Mode)`, `agents_md_expected(Mode)`.
   `cli/src/init.rs` imports and uses them — the sentinel strings exist in exactly
   one place in the codebase.

2. `core::doctor` defines `Status { Ok, Warn, Fail, Info }`,
   `Check { group, name, status, detail, hint }`, `Report { mode: Mode, checks }`,
   and `diagnose(root: &Path, force: ModeChoice) -> Report`. Pure / read-only.

3. Mode resolution via `active_mode(root, force)` (T03): no flag → auto-detect;
   `--explore`/`--standard` force.

4. **Universal checks** run for every project: `grove_version` (Info);
   `config_present` (Ok/Fail/Info); `legacy_explore_json` (Warn if drift);
   four harness sub-checks using `core::harness` constants; `registry_root`;
   `grammar_cache`; `project_languages`; `lock_integrity`.

5. **Harness checks** compare on-disk state to `harness::expected_*` → Fail on
   drift naming the mismatched file; hint instructs `grove init --as <mode>`.

6. **`grove.lock` sha256 verify** via T06 `verify_lock`: present → per-grammar
   Match→Ok / Mismatch→Fail / Missing→Warn; absent → Warn.

7. **Explore-mode checks** (McpLlm only): `explore_config_valid`,
   `provider_reachable`, `model_served`, `allowed_tools_known`, `tap_config`.

8. `cli/src/main.rs` gains `Cmd::Doctor { path, explore, standard }` + global
   `--json`; renders grouped ✓/⚠/✗ table or machine-readable JSON; sets exit
   code from `Report::ok()` — 0 when ok (warnings allowed), 1 on any Fail.

9. `Report::ok()` is `true` when only `Warn`/`Info` checks are present (no Fail).
   A warn-only run exits 0 under both output formats.

10. `cargo build` warning-clean; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean;
    `cargo test` green; `--help` documents `doctor`.

---

content.thinking:
All ACs are verified. Let me now summarize the findings:

**AC1** ✅: `core::harness` defines `GROVE_START`, `GROVE_END`, `MCP_SERVER_KEY`, `expected_mcp_args(Mode)`, `expected_claude_marker(Mode)`, `agents_md_expected(Mode)`. `cli/src/init.rs` imports from `grove_core::harness` with alias (`GROVE_START as CLAUDE_START` etc.) — sentinel strings in exactly one place.
**AC2** ✅: `core::doctor` provides the `Status` enum with variants for different check outcomes, a `Check` struct capturing group, name, status, detail, and hint, a `Report` struct holding mode and checks, and a `diagnose` function that takes a root path and mode choice to generate the report — all read-only operations.
**AC3** ✅: Mode resolution happens through `active_mode(root, force)` where force comes from `ModeChoice` variants tied to `--explore` and `--standard` CLI flags.
**AC4** ✅: Universal checks are in place, including `grove_version` as an info check and `config_present` as an ok check.

The remaining checks cover legacy explore JSON drift detection, four harness-related sub-checks, registry root validation, grammar cache verification, project language detection, and lock file integrity validation. Harness checks leverage expected MCP arguments, Claude markers, and agents markdown structure, failing on drift with a hint to run `grove init --as <mode>`. Lock integrity delegates to the registry's verification logic, mapping absent locks to warnings, matches to ok, mismatches to failures, and missing entries to warnings. The explore mode checks validate the config, test provider reachability, verify model availability, confirm tool knowledge, and check tap configuration.

The CLI command accepts a path and flags for explore and standard modes, with global JSON output support and grouped results by status level. The report determines exit codes based on whether any failures exist—warnings and info alone return success. I've verified the code compiles cleanly with clippy, all 303 tests pass in release mode, and the help documentation is properly tested.

All acceptance criteria check out. Time to document the validation report.

content.text: All acceptance criteria verified. Now let me write the validation report:

arguments.content:
# VALIDATION REPORT — GROVE-S03-T07 (standalone review)

**Task:** grove doctor — `core::doctor` report + CLI verb (checks, `--json`, exit code)
**Verdict:** Approved

---

## Method

Full test suite executed (`cargo test --release --locked`), `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` run, and each acceptance criterion traced directly to implementation code and test assertions.

---

## Acceptance Criteria Checklist

### AC1 — `core::harness` single source of truth ✅

`core/src/harness.rs` defines `GROVE_START`, `GROVE_END`, `MCP_SERVER_KEY` as `pub const`, and exports `expected_mcp_args(Mode)`, `expected_claude_marker(Mode)`, `agents_md_expected(Mode)`. `cli/src/init.rs` line 16 imports all three constants via `use grove_core::harness::{GROVE_START as CLAUDE_START, GROVE_END as CLAUDE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY}` — no local duplicate constants remain.

### AC2 — `core::doctor` type surface ✅

`core/src/doctor.rs` defines:
- `pub enum Status { Ok, Warn, Fail, Info }` (verified source)
- `pub struct Check { group, name, status, detail, hint }` (verified source)
- `pub struct Report { mode: Mode, checks: Vec<Check> }` (verified source)
- `pub fn diagnose(root: &Path, force: ModeChoice) -> Report` (verified source) — pure/read-only; no writes inside

### AC3 — Mode resolution via `active_mode` ✅

`cli/src/main.rs` Doctor dispatch converts `--standard`→`ModeChoice::ForceStandard`, `--explore`→`ModeChoice::ForceExplore`, neither→`ModeChoice::None`, then calls `doctor::diagnose(&path, force)`. `diagnose` calls `active_mode(root, force)` internally. Precedence: `--standard` beats `--explore` when both are passed.

### AC4 — Universal checks run for every project ✅

`diagnose` emits exactly the required universal checks in order:
1. `grove_version` (Info)
2. `config_present` (Ok/Fail/Info)
3. `legacy_explore_json` (Warn if drift)
4. `harness_mcp_json` via `check_harness_mcp_json`
5. `harness_claude_md` via `check_harness_claude_md`
6. `harness_agents_md` via `check_harness_agents_md`
7. `harness_serve_surface` via `check_harness_serve_surface`
8. `registry_root` (Ok/Fail)
9. `grammar_cache` (Ok/Warn)
10. `project_languages` (Ok/Warn/Info)
11. `lock_integrity` (Ok/Fail/Warn)

### AC5 — Harness checks → Fail on drift with hint ✅

All three file-level harness check functions use `harness::expected_mcp_args/expected_claude_marker/agents_md_expected`. On any mismatch, `status: Status::Fail` with `hint: Some(format!("grove init --as {}", mode_name(mode)))`. Unit test `harness_matrix_clean_fixtures_all_ok` validates all 5 modes produce Ok on correct fixtures; drift tests `mcp_mode_with_explore_args_in_mcp_json_is_fail`, `mcp_mode_with_explore_marker_in_claude_md_is_fail`, `mcp_llm_mode_without_agents_md_is_warn`, `grammars_mode_with_grove_block_in_claude_md_is_warn` validate specific drift scenarios.

### AC6 — `grove.lock` sha256 verify via T06 `verify_lock` ✅

`lock_integrity` check calls `registry::verify_lock(&lock_path)`: `Ok(None)` (absent) → Warn; per-entry: `Match` → Ok, `Mismatch` → Fail, `Missing` → Warn; overall status is Fail if any Mismatch, else Warn if any Missing, else Ok. Unit test `lock_integrity_absent_lockfile_is_warn` confirms absent → Warn (not Fail).

### AC7 — Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only) ✅

`diagnose` gates `explore_checks()` on `mode == Mode::McpLlm`. `explore_checks` produces: `explore_config_valid`, `provider_reachable`, `model_served`, `allowed_tools_known`, `tap_config`. `HealthError::Unreachable` and `HealthError::ModelMissing` hints surfaced verbatim. Unit tests `explore_config_absent_is_fail` and `provider_unreachable_is_fail` validate edge cases.

### AC8 — `Cmd::Doctor` + `--json` + exit code ✅

`cli/src/main.rs` `Cmd::Doctor { path, explore, standard }` is present. Global `--json` flag at line 24 drives both output paths. Human output groups checks by `group` with ✓/⚠/✗/· icons. JSON output includes `path`, `mode`, `ok`, `checks[]`, `summary` counts. Exit code: `if !report.ok() { std::process::exit(1); }` after both output paths. Integration tests `doctor_universal_clean_exits_zero`, `doctor_json_output_is_valid`, and `doctor_fail_exits_nonzero` (both human and json) confirm both formats.

### AC9 — `Report::ok()` true when only Warn/Info; exits 0 ✅

```rust
pub fn ok(&self) -> bool {
    self.checks.iter().all(|c| !matches!(c.status, Status::Fail))
}
```
Only `Status::Fail` causes `ok()` to return false. Unit test `warn_only_report_exits_zero` builds a report with one Warn and one Info check and asserts `report.ok()` is true.

### AC10 — Build clean, clippy clean, tests green ✅

- `cargo clippy --release -- -D warnings`: **clean** (no warnings or errors)
- `cargo test --release --locked`: **303 tests, 0 failed** (177 core + 92 CLI unit + 33 integration + 1 doc-test)
- Integration test `doctor_help_documents_verb` confirms `--help` includes `doctor`

---

## Edge Cases and Boundary Conditions

| Scenario | Verified By |
|---|---|
| Absent grove.lock → lock_integrity is Warn, not Fail | unit: `lock_integrity_absent_lockfile_is_warn` |
| Warn-only report → exits 0 | unit: `warn_only_report_exits_zero` |
| Drift in `.mcp.json` → Fail with `grove init --as` hint | unit: `mcp_mode_with_explore_args_in_mcp_json_is_fail` |
| McpLlm mode without `AGENTS.md` → Warn | unit: `mcp_llm_mode_without_agents_md_is_warn` |
| Explore checks absent when mode ≠ McpLlm | integration: `doctor_universal_clean_exits_zero` (Mcp fixture, no explore group) |
| `explore_config_valid` absent → Fail + early return | unit: `explore_config_absent_is_fail` |
| Provider unreachable → `model_served` is Info (skipped) | unit: `provider_unreachable_is_fail` |
| Drift exits nonzero under JSON output | integration: `doctor_fail_exits_nonzero` (json branch) |

---

## Regression Check

All 303 pre-existing + new tests pass. No regressions detected.

---

## Validation Result: **Approved** ✅

**Task:** GROVE-S03-T07 — `grove doctor` core::doctor + CLI verb

### Test Evidence
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`: **clean**
- `cargo test --release --locked`: **303 tests, 0 failed** (177 core + 92 CLI unit + 33 integration + 1 doc-test)

### All 10 ACs Pass

| AC | Result | Evidence |
